sizing_rule.note: Apply shrink parameter as multiplier to average trade size to scale position sizing. Shrink=2 doubles average trade; shrink=0.5 halves it. Negative shrink values model conversion of unprofitable systems (avg_trade<0) to profitable scenarios. Used for sensitivity analysis of how trade magnitude changes affect optimal position sizing.

source_rule_text: Use a shrink parameter to scale average trade size up or down, allowing analysis of how doubling (or halving) average trades affects position sizing, or to model the effects of converting unprofitable systems into profitable ones.
